registerPool(address).details: Callable only by owner. The pool's tokenA/tokenB are read from the      contract to ensure the canonical key matches the pool's own ordering.

params.amountIn: Exact amount of tokenIn to spend.
params.amountOutMin: Minimum amount of tokenOut to receive (slippage guard).
params.deadline: Unix timestamp after which the transaction reverts.

params.amountInMax: Maximum amount of tokenIn to spend (slippage guard).
params.amountOut: Exact amount of tokenOut to receive.
params.deadline: Unix timestamp after which the transaction reverts.

getAmountIn(uint256,address,address).notice: Quote: how much tokenIn is needed to receive exactly `amountOut`?         Inverse of the GoodPool AMM formula (0.3% fee):           amountIn = ceil(amountOut * resIn * 10000 / ((resOut - amountOut) * 9970))

getAmountOut(uint256,address,address).notice: Quote: how much tokenOut for `amountIn` of tokenIn?

getPool(address,address).notice: Look up the pool for a given token pair (order-independent).

pools(bytes32).notice: Pool registry: canonical pair key → GoodPool address.         Key is keccak256(abi.encodePacked(lowerAddr, higherAddr)).

registerPool(address).notice: Register a GoodPool for a token pair.

removePool(address,address).notice: Remove a pool from the registry.

swapExactTokensForTokens(uint256,uint256.notice: Swap an exact input amount for as many output tokens as possible.

swapTokensForExactTokens(uint256,uint256.notice: Receive an exact output amount by spending as few input tokens as possible.

documentation.text: @notice Minimal read interface for GoodPool (x*y=k AMM).

@notice Pool registry: canonical pair key → GoodPool address.
         Key is keccak256(abi.encodePacked(lowerAddr, higherAddr)).
@notice Remove a pool from the registry.
@notice Look up the pool for a given token pair (order-independent).
@notice Quote: how much tokenOut for `amountIn` of tokenIn?
@dev Canonical key for a token pair: keccak256(lower, higher).

@dev Return (reserveIn, reserveOut) sorted relative to tokenIn.
      GoodPool always stores the lower-address token as reserveA.

@title GoodSwapRouter
 @notice UniV2-style router for the GoodSwap constant-product AMM.
         Users approve the router (not individual pools) and call
         swapExactTokensForTokens / swapTokensForExactTokens. The router
         looks up the registered GoodPool for the given token pair, executes
         the swap, and forwards output tokens to `to`.
         Fee architecture: GoodPool collects 0.3% on input and routes 33.33%
         of that fee to the UBIFeeSplitter automatically (no router action
         needed). The router only handles routing and slippage enforcement.
 @dev Only direct (single-hop) swaps are supported. Multi-hop paths with
      more than 2 tokens are not implemented.
